medical_history.chief_complaint: Thrombocytopenia detected during treatment for neonatal jaundice
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Thrombocytopenia persisted, with a platelet count ranging from 1.7 x 10^9/L to 16.2 x 10^9/L. Waddling gait began in middle adolescence and muscle weakness gradually progressed, particularly in the proximal lower limbs. At the age of 20 years, proximal muscle weakness was noted, particularly in the neck flexor and iliopsoas. Serum creatine kinase levels ranged from 300 to 600 IU/L. Muscle biopsy showed increased rimmed vacuoles in approximately 1% of myofibers. He has been wheelchair-bound since the age of 24 years.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Proteinuria and hematuria detected at the age of 7 years, progressed to chronic renal failure requiring periodic dialysis therapy. Mildly decreased cardiac function accompanied by mitral insufficiency from the age of 22 years.
medical_history.family_history: Parents and other family members have no medical history of hematological or neuromuscular disorders.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological examination at the age of 20 years showed proximal muscle weakness, particularly in the neck flexor and iliopsoas.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Ranging from 1.7 x 10^9/L to 16.2 x 10^9/L (Patient 1), 1.1 x 10^9/L to 9.0 x 10^9/L (Patient 2)

laboratory_examinations.date: Age of 8 years (Patient 1), Age of 5 years (Patient 2)
laboratory_examinations.findings: Megakaryocytes morphologically normal and increased in number (281/mm3 in Patient 1), normal in number and morphology (Patient 2)
Elevated (207.3 ng/10^7 cells in Patient 1, 119 ng/10^7 cells in Patient 2)
Identified compound heterozygous variants in FLNB and GNE
Revealed three regions with recessively inherited deletions of genomic copy number

radiographic_examinations.findings: Remarkable atrophy in the paraspinal muscles and moderate patchy atrophy in the adductor magnus, soleus and tibialis posterior muscles

other_tests.changes_over_time: Progressed to chronic renal failure requiring periodic dialysis therapy

other_tests.findings: Increased rimmed vacuoles in approximately 1% of myofibers, located predominantly in atrophic fibers. Necrotic and regenerating fibers were rare, and no inflammatory cellular infiltration was observed
Identified two missense mutations: c.1807G > C; p.V603L and c.2215G > A; p.G739S

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: GNE myopathy associated with congenital thrombocytopenia
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of myopathy with rimmed vacuoles and congenital thrombocytopenia in two siblings, confirmed by genetic testing which identified compound heterozygous GNE mutations (p.V603L and p.G739S). The thrombocytopenia was characterized by shortened platelet lifetime and elevated platelet-associated IgG (PAIgG). Exome sequencing and array CGH did not identify pathogenic variants in known genes for thrombocytopenia, suggesting the thrombocytopenia may be associated with the GNE mutations and resultant hyposialylation of platelets.
